channel.title: The Atlas on The City
channel.description: Recent content in The Atlas on The City

item.title: The Tangle
item.description: Nobody realised what Captain Green really did. They saw a couple square blocks become wild, fed by Wyld Magic and insanity. That wasn’t true, it was largner, much larger. The small space seen became a larger space of several square block, each holding many times what it would have held for physical space. The blocks that vanished were swallowed up by the plants that filled it, tens of years of nature taking over compressed into a short perioud. The sleepers, well they diddn’t realize a thing.

item.title: Keziah Mason Cistern
item.description: A former drinking water reservoir built in the early 1900s. Lauded at the time for the innovated design of catch basins, water capture, and stunning architecual feature the construction ran well over budget and schedule. Designed by the noted architect Antonio Ricci as one of his first projects after leaving the tutalage of Neils Thorp. Rather than the usual rectangle with pillar construction, the straight lines with obtuse angles were considered cutting edge at flow managment at the time. The ornate pillars of the Greek Revivial Style and the ornamentation give it an odd feeling when the water is low enough that people can access it. Awakened or aware people often report being uncomfortable and attribute it to the cool dampness of the cistern.

item.title: The Drains
item.description:
“People say the sewers only carry rain and waste. But sometimes, late at night, something down there reminds you the stories about the alligators were never meant to be jokes.”

For most people, what goes down the drains never comes back. In the City, what goes down the drain can come back to haunt you or crawl out to hunt you. Gators may be the least of your worries.

The drains in the City were inspired by many sources. Stories, the Paris Catacombs, urban legends about New York, and a wastewater management class in college. IT also played a large part.

item.title: The Docks
item.description: Another test posts, testing tags added to an archetype.
